raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 2,
    "R1.7": 1,
    "RX.1": 1,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The section claims to measure per-user intent alignment, but the benchmark actually measures majority-vote labels, which is a corpus-average prior.",
    "R1.7": "The citation and scholarship are not properly engaged with; the paper does not provide sufficient information about the source of the logs, licensing, anchoring procedure, or inter-annotator agreement.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty disclosure is lacking; the section presents all output as equally confident without acknowledging potential uncertainty in user intent alignment.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence is a concern; the paper does not clearly define what constitutes an 'ambiguous query' and how it is operationalized in the benchmark.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance is a problem; a system that claims to serve individual user intent but is actually trained and evaluated to return the majority interpretation will systematically underserve minority-interpretation users.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability are lacking; the paper does not provide sufficient information about the data collection process, including inter-annotator agreement and statistical significance."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.95,
    "RX.3": 0.85,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The section claims to measure per-user intent alignment, but the benchmark actually measures majority-vote labels, which is a corpus-average prior.
rationale.R1.7: The citation and scholarship are not properly engaged with; the paper does not provide sufficient information about the source of the logs, licensing, anchoring procedure, or inter-annotator agreement.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is lacking; the section presents all output as equally confident without acknowledging potential uncertainty in user intent alignment.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is a concern; the paper does not clearly define what constitutes an 'ambiguous query' and how it is operationalized in the benchmark.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is a problem; a system that claims to serve individual user intent but is actually trained and evaluated to return the majority interpretation will systematically underserve minority-interpretation users.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are lacking; the paper does not provide sufficient information about the data collection process, including inter-annotator agreement and statistical significance.
